allegation: Taking a vehicle without consent, contrary to section 12 of the Theft Act 1968

cpsChase.label: anpr image export
cpsChase.draft: Please provide anpr image export or confirm in writing why it is not available.

courtLine: The defence asks the court to record that ANPR image export, audit trail, and keeper/vehicle attribution material remain outstanding.
clientSummaryPreview:
CLIENT-SAFE SUMMARY
(not for court or CPS)

We are reviewing the papers in your case (Maya Singh). This is early-stage — nothing is final until we have full disclosure and your instructions. An ANPR hit table may be on the papers, but ANPR image export, national audit trail, and keeper/vehicle attribution responses remain outstanding. A hit table alone does not prove keeper identity.

[CaseBrain — client-safe summary. Evidence state: provisional. Not for court or CPS use.]
doNotOverstate: Do not treat an ANPR hit table alone as proof of vehicle attribution or keeper identity.

proofReceipts.outputLine: The defence asks the court to record that ANPR image export, audit trail, and keeper/vehicle attribution material remain outstanding.
Please provide anpr image export or confirm in writing why it is not available.
Please provide national anpr audit trail or confirm in writing why it is not available.
Please provide vehicle keeper response or confirm in writing why it is not available.
